summary: Gruber criticizes Tim Cook for appearing alongside Trump at Apple's Mac Pro assembly plant in Austin, arguing that while previous engagement with Trump was defensible, this crossed a line into implicit support for Trump's re-election. The staged photo-op was used as a Trump campaign ad filled with false claims, including that Trump opened a new facility when Apple has manufactured there since 2013. Gruber notes that Apple's Mac Pro production remains in the U.S. not because of Trump's trade policies but because Trump granted Apple a tariff exemption. He expresses dismay at Apple's packaging reveal happening in a poorly-shot White House propaganda video and calls it a low moment in Apple's history.
key_points: Participating in Trump's tech council and private engagement doesn't imply support, but appearing in a staged campaign photo-op does
Trump falsely claimed he opened a new Apple facility when Mac Pro assembly has been at the same Austin plant since 2013
Apple kept Mac Pro production in the U.S. not because of Trump's trade policies but because Trump granted a tariff exemption from his own tariffs
The Mac Pro packaging was unveiled not through Apple's usual careful marketing but through a poorly-shot White House propaganda video
Cook's silent presence during Trump's tirade against the press and impeachment deflection represents a troubling moment for Apple's brand
money_quotes: Appearing alongside Trump at an Apple facility in a staged photo-op is implicit support for Trump and his re-election.
This wasn't a promotion for the Mac Pro or its assembly plant. It was a promotion for Trump.
But Cook went into this knowing that this is how Trump would play it — a big pile of nonsensical horseshit all the way down.

This is how Apple chose to unveil the packaging for the Mac Pro — in a poorly-shot overexposed propaganda video by the White House, scored with bombastic music that sounds like it came from an SNL parody of a Michael Bay film.
Think about how it feels to work on that team at Apple.
A low moment in Apple's proud history, and a sadly iconic moment for Tim Cook.
I hope avoiding those tariffs is worth it.
key_insight: Tim Cook crossed the line from pragmatic engagement with Trump to implicit endorsement by appearing in what became a campaign ad built entirely on false claims about Apple's manufacturing.
